claim: Every spawn-log entry shall carry isolation (worktree|shared) and mutating (bool) fields, defaulting to shared/false when the caller omits them.
why: Not yet ported in the Go port. Wave 5 (measurable slices of discipline): the historical spawn-log already recorded WHO/WHAT/WHEN (R-task-spawn-log-runtime) but not the isolation posture under which a sub-agent ran, so a parallel-mutating-agent hazard had no trace at all. The historical writer was tools/spawn_agent.py, whose freeze under R-speculative-aspects-frozen was partially lifted by explicit steward act (GO given for Wave 5) to add two additive CLI flags (--isolation, --mutating) and matching log fields, backward-compatible with every pre-existing caller. In the Go port spawn-agent is Declared (not ported) in methodology.Tools -- there is no spawn-log writer, no spawn-log.jsonl, and no isolation/mutating fields, because no sub-agent spawning machinery exists at all (R-claude-md-consolidates-when-single-agent: one operator, zero active sub-agents). The writer half (this atom) and the honest reader half (R-parallel-mutating-agents-use-worktree, which can only check log-internal consistency, not runtime concurrency) are both unported together, since both depend on the unported spawn machinery. PROSE (not ENFORCED): no enforcer exists in the Go port.
